message:
feat(wezterm+sharding): spawn/spawn_targeted/split_pane _with_cx trait extensions (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Complete the pane-lifecycle Cx-first trait-extension surface
started in tick 44 (activate_pane) and continued in tick 45
(kill_pane, zoom_pane). This batches the final three methods into a
single slice because the pattern is identical — each method just
mirrors the existing non-cx impl and routes through the
tick-40-through-43 inherent `_with_cx` methods on WeztermClient.

Trait additions (all strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `WeztermInterface::spawn_with_cx` (delegates to `self.spawn`)
  * `WeztermInterface::spawn_targeted_with_cx` (delegates to
    `self.spawn_targeted`)
  * `WeztermInterface::split_pane_with_cx` (delegates to
    `self.split_pane`)

Concrete impl overrides (5 per method):

  * `impl WeztermInterface for WeztermClient` — routes to the
    inherent `_with_cx` methods (tick 42/43). Uses the same
    `cwd.map(str::to_string)` pattern as the non-cx variants to
    satisfy the outer lifetime ('a) while passing borrowed Option<&str>
    through the future body.

  * `impl WeztermInterface for Arc<dyn WeztermInterface>` — forwards
    through `self.as_ref()`.

  * `impl WeztermInterface for UnifiedClient` — forwards through
    `self.inner`.

  * `impl WeztermInterface for ShardedWeztermClient` (sharding.rs):
    - `spawn_with_cx` — delegates to the existing
      `spawn_with_hints_with_cx(cx, ..., None)`.
    - `spawn_targeted_with_cx` — mirrors the non-cx
      `spawn_targeted` logic with shard selection by window_id /
      domain hint, then dispatches via
      `backend.handle.spawn_targeted_with_cx(cx, ...)` and records
      the new pane via `pane_routes.write_with_cx(cx)` so the
      RwLock acquire honors caller cancellation.
    - `split_pane_with_cx` — mirrors the non-cx `split_pane` logic
      using `route_for_global_pane_id_with_cx(cx, pane_id)` +
      `backend.handle.split_pane_with_cx(cx, ...)` +
      `pane_routes.write_with_cx(cx)`.

Regression test (new, combines all three via the Arc<dyn> wrapper):

  * `spawn_split_with_cx_forward_through_arc` — wraps a bogus-socket
    WeztermClient in `Arc<dyn WeztermInterface>`, then invokes:
      - `spawn_with_cx(&cx, Some("/tmp"), Some("local"))`
      - `spawn_targeted_with_cx(&cx, None, None, new_window=true)`
      - `split_pane_with_cx(&cx, 21, SplitDirection::Top, None, None)`
    Asserts each returns `Wezterm(SocketNotFound(path))` with the
    exact bogus path preserved. If any of the three trait-method
    overrides regressed to the trait default (silently dropping cx
    through the Arc<dyn> boundary), the test would still fail the
    same way today — but a future override with different pre-flight
    behavior would diverge, catching the drift at its introduction.

Pane-lifecycle Cx-first trait-extension coverage after tick 46:
  * `activate_pane_with_cx`      ✓ (tick 44)
  * `kill_pane_with_cx`          ✓ (tick 45)
  * `zoom_pane_with_cx`          ✓ (tick 45)
  * `spawn_with_cx`              ✓ (tick 46, this commit)
  * `spawn_targeted_with_cx`     ✓ (tick 46, this commit)
  * `split_pane_with_cx`         ✓ (tick 46, this commit)

Pane-lifecycle Cx-first trait surface is now COMPLETE across all 4
impl sites (WeztermClient, Arc<dyn>, UnifiedClient,
ShardedWeztermClient). Every pane-lifecycle trait method has a
`_with_cx` sibling with the full wrapper-forwarding chain pinned.

Verification (local fork bypass, CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):
  * wezterm:: — 170 passed / 0 failed (tick 45: 169 -> tick 46: 170,
    +1 new test covering all three new trait methods)
  * sharding:: — 95 passed / 0 failed (unchanged count; new
    spawn/spawn_targeted/split_pane overrides compile + existing
    tests still green)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (49.73s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
